Contact Preet Kaur Gill MP Labour and Co-operative MP for Bartley Green, Edgbaston, Harborne, Quinton & North Edgbaston Latest News Contact Labour MP for Birmingham Edgbaston Since 2017, I have been the MP for Birmingham, Edgbaston. It has been the privilege of my life to represent the place where I was born and raised in Parliament. For casework inquiries, please fill in the adjacent form, or call 0121 392 8426, or email preet.gill.mp@parliament.uk.​ How can I help you? I would like to sign up to Preet's Weekly Newsletter I agree for Preet Kaur Gill to handle my data as set out in my privacy policy Submit Thanks for your message! I'll be in touch soon. MY RECENT WEEKLY UPDATES Preet Kaur Gill MP - My Weekly Update - 1st May 2026 2 days ago Preet Kaur Gill MP - My Weekly Update - 24th April 2026 Apr 23 Preet Kaur Gill MP - My Weekly Update - 10th April 2026 Apr 9 Preet Kaur Gill MP - My Weekly Update - 3rd April 2026 Apr 1 Preet Kaur Gill MP - My Weekly Update - 13th March 2026 Mar 12 60,000 Casework requests answered 2,750 Questions in Parliament About Preet I am the Member of Parliament for Birmingham, Edgbaston, PPS to Science, Innovation and Technology Secretary Liz Kendall, and the UK’s first female Sikh MP. Born and raised in the Birmingham, I was a social worker and a Councillor, and Cabinet Member for Public Health and Protection prior to entering Parliament. I am Chair of the Co-operative Party Parliamentary Group, and Vice-President of the Local Government Association. ​ In 2018, I featured on Birmingham City University’s Brummies Who Inspire and was presented an award for Sikh Woman of Substance by the Sikh Women’s Alliance. In 2020, I was named MP of the Year by the Patchwork Foundation. ​ I have campaigned for stronger regulation of the exempt accommodation sector, leasehold reform, measures to tackle fuel poverty, offensive weapons, curbs on 3D-printed firearms and fairness in data collection. IN PARLIAMENT PPS to Liz Kendall In September 2025, I was appointed as the Parliamentary Private Secretary (PPS) to Liz Kendall, Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology. ​ Between July 2024 and September 2025, I served as the PPS to Jonathan Reynolds, Secretary of State for Business and Trade. Prior to this, I was the Shadow Minister for Primary Care and Public Health, where I worked with GPs, pharmacists and dentists – the front door of our health service – to build an NHS fit for the future. ​ I also served as Shadow Secretary of State for International Development. In this role, I championed the UN’s Sustainable Development Goals, advocated for ending poverty, improving global health, gender equality, pandemic preparedness and a new model for international climate finance. In the news The Telegraph - Why I'm Blue Labour ,6th January 2026 Mace - Preet Kaur Gill, January 2026 BBC Politics Live - 28th January 2026 ITV Central - Birmingham Bin Strikes, 28th January 2026 Times Radio - Keir Starmer & Birmingham Bin Strikes, 10th February 2026 In the News Preet Kaur Gill MP for Birmingham Edgbaston covering Bartley Green, Edgbaston, Harborne, Quinton and North Edgbaston Privacy Policy Contact Me Write to Preet House of Commons London SW1A 0AA ​ Phone Preet 0121 392 8426 ​ Email Preet preet.gill.mp@parliament.uk Sign up to my email newsletter Preet Kaur Gill will contact you about her work using the data you have supplied. You can opt out at any time. The privacy policy is here Submit Thanks for subscribing bottom of page

Contact All Posts Weekly Updates Constituency News Parliamentary News Useful information Letters Media Have your say: How should we spent £20 million investment in Woodgate, Bartley Green? Preet Kaur Gill MP for Birmingham Edgbaston is calling on Woodgate and Bartley Green residents to share how they think the £20 million Pride in Place investment should be spent. After years of campaigning Preet Kaur Gill has secured £20 million for Woodgate and Bartley Green as part of the Pride in Place programme. The funding is allocated as £20 million spread out across ten years. Apr 16 2 min read Preet Kaur Gill MP urges parents, schools and caterers in Birmingham Edgbaston to have their say on new school food standards Preet Kaur Gill, MP for Birmingham Edgbaston has welcomed the government’s plans to update the School Food Standards for the first time in over a decade and urged local people to contribute to the consultation. The move comes after polling revealed three quarters of parents are concerned by the food their children are eating, as one in three children are leaving primary school overweight or obese.
